subject: Re: Boxnet vs 3090
body:
Hi All, 

Just a few (probably useless) updates. Retraining boxnet does not work. 

On the bright side, M still seems to be running fine with the 3090 cards. 
It does suffer from some of the same symptoms as described by Teige for 
Warp (in a different post), i.e. very slow at 'preparing refinement 
requisites', with M going to 'not responding' every minute or so. The same 
behaviour occurs at the end of the refinement, but the volumes coming out 
look OK. 

Best wishes,
Tom
Op donderdag 25 februari 2021 om 00:52:15 UTC schreef teig...@gmail.com:

> Hey everyone,
>
> Is anyone out there using RTX 3090 GPUs and *not* having any problems with 
> boxnet or denoising?
>
> It would be really helpful to know if there is somebody using 3090s 
> without a problem - to falsify this n=3(?) finding that they cause a 
> problem with picking and denoising.
>
> Thanks
> Teige
>
> On 25 Feb 2021, at 02:47, tom.de...@gmail.com <tom.de...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Simon,
>
> I'm seeing the same here. 
>
>  Motioncorr and CTF estimation work fine, but particle picking and 
> denoising don't work properly on a workstation with Geforce RTX 3090 cards. 
> The same dataset runs fine on a workstation with 2080 cards. I'll try to 
> retrain the picking/denoising to see if that helps and post the outcome 
> here.
>
> Cheers,
> Tom
>
>
>
> Op zondag 17 januari 2021 om 15:37:02 UTC schreef simoner...@gmail.com:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> Is anyone else experiencing problem with denoising and particle picking 
>> on the Geforce 3090? Motioncorr and CTF works very well though. 
>>
>>  I have another workstation with two Ti2080s running fine. 
>>
>> Thanks and best 
>> Simon
>>
>
> --
